role: Speaker of Parliament (2008–2020); Former chief nuclear negotiator
bio: Ali Larijani is the longest-serving speaker in the history of the Islamic Republic, holding the position from 2008 to 2020. He previously served as Iran's chief nuclear negotiator (2005–2007) and secretary of the [Supreme National Security Council](/orgs/supreme-national-security-council). He was disqualified from the 2021 presidential election by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council). His brother [Sadegh Larijani](/people/sadegh-larijani) served as head of the judiciary.
editorial_notes: Born in Najaf to an Iranian clerical family. Disqualified from 2021 presidential election.
